00038 
00039 package java.lang;
00040 
00041 import java.lang.Double;
00042 
00054 public final class Float
00055 {
00060     public static final float MAX_VALUE = 3.4028235e+38f;
00061 
00066     public static final float MIN_VALUE = 1.4e-45f;
00067 
00071     public static final float NEGATIVE_INFINITY = -1.0f / 0.0f;
00072 
00076     public static final float POSITIVE_INFINITY = 1.0f / 0.0f;
00077 
00081     public static final float NaN = 0.0f / 0.0f;
00082 
00088     private final float value;
00089 
00096     public Float(float value)
00097     {
00098         this.value = value;
00099     }
00100 
00107     public Float(double value)
00108     {
00109         this.value = (float) value;
00110     }
00111 
00142     public native static String toString(float f);
00143 
00154     public static Float valueOf(String s)
00155     {
00156         return new Float(parseFloat(s));
00157     }
00158 
00205     public static float parseFloat(String str)
00206     {
00207         // XXX Rounding parseDouble() causes some errors greater than 1 ulp from
00208         // the infinitely precise decimal.
00209         return (float) Double.parseDouble(str);
00210     }
00211 
00219     public static boolean isNaN(float v)
00220     {
00221         // This works since NaN != NaN is the only reflexive inequality
00222         // comparison which returns true.
00223         return v != v;
00224     }
00225 
00234     public static boolean isInfinite(float v)
00235     {
00236         return v == POSITIVE_INFINITY || v == NEGATIVE_INFINITY;
00237     }
00238 
00245     public boolean isNaN()
00246     {
00247         return isNaN(value);
00248     }
00249 
00257     public boolean isInfinite()
00258     {
00259         return isInfinite(value);
00260     }
00261 
00270     public String toString()
00271     {
00272         return toString(value);
00273     }
00274 
00280     public byte byteValue()
00281     {
00282         return (byte) value;
00283     }
00284 
00291     public short shortValue()
00292     {
00293         return (short) value;
00294     }
00295 
00301     public int intValue()
00302     {
00303         return (int) value;
00304     }
00305 
00311     public long longValue()
00312     {
00313         return (long) value;
00314     }
00315 
00321     public float floatValue()
00322     {
00323         return value;
00324     }
00325 
00331     public double doubleValue()
00332     {
00333         return value;
00334     }
00335 
00343     public int hashCode()
00344     {
00345         return floatToIntBits(value);
00346     }
00347 
00362     public boolean equals(Object obj)
00363     {
00364         if (! (obj instanceof Float))
00365             return false;
00366 
00367         float f = ((Float) obj).value;
00368 
00369         // Avoid call to native method. However, some implementations, like gcj,
00370         // are better off using floatToIntBits(value) == floatToIntBits(f).
00371         // Check common case first, then check NaN and 0.
00372         if (value == f)
00373             return (value != 0) || (1 / value == 1 / f);
00374 
00375         return isNaN(value) && isNaN(f);
00376     }
00377 
00391     public native static int floatToIntBits(float value);
00392 
00404     public native static float intBitsToFloat(int bits);
00405 }
